title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 20 lines).
summary_text: - High: [freee-client.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/bookkeeping/scripts/freee-client.mjs#L155) now hard-fails unless `FREEE_CLIENT_ID` and `FREEE_CLIENT_SECRET` exist in env or macOS Keychain. The previous code passed these opportunistically and still allowed `freee-mcp` to run with its own cached token/config. That is a real regression for machines with valid freee auth but no those specific keychain entries, and it also makes the script effectively macOS-only. - Medium: [context-monitor.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/context-monitor.sh#L17) raises the trigger from `30` to `60` while the hook still describes itself as the near-limit safety mechanism (`~85%`, `handover_suggested`). If other automation or operator behavior relies on this warning, it now fires much later and can miss the intended prevention window. Assumption: I reviewed the tracked staged/unstaged diff; untracked files were not part of the diff. No new obvious secret-leak or command-injection issue stood out in the tracked changes.
